records.ety: [OE. erli, erliche, AS. ǣrlīce; ǣr sooner + līc like. See Ere, and Like.]

defs: Soon; in good season; seasonably; betimes; as, come early.
quotes: Those that me early shall find me.
You must wake and call me early.

defs: In advance of the usual or appointed time; in good season; prior in time; among or near the first; -- opposed to late; as, the early bird; an early spring; early fruit.
quotes: Early and provident fear is the mother of safety.
The doorsteps and threshold with the early grass springing up about them.

defs: Coming in the first part of a period of time, or among the first of successive acts, events, etc.
quotes: Seen in life's early morning sky.
The forms of its earlier manhood.
The earliest poem he composed was in his seventeenth summer.
